title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 25)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington homes sold include a $875,000 three-family flat and a $750,000 raised ranch.
Acton featured a Cape Cod home at 2 Huron Road selling for $1,325,000.
A condo at 397 Commonwealth Ave., Boston, sold for $6,725,000.

title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, convicted for 1989 aggravated rapes in Framingham after evading police for over 30 years.
Modern DNA technology identified Gale as the suspect, leading to his arrest following a car chase in Los Angeles.
Gale will be sentenced on March 30 for charges including aggravated rape, kidnapping, and armed robbery.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson was introduced as Providence's new men's basketball head coach amid efforts to revamp the struggling program.
Hodgson jokingly referenced his wife, Jordan, to highlight his recruiting abilities during his introductory press conference.
He previously led South Florida to a 25-9 record and an NCAA tournament bid in the 2025-26 season.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alex Castrichini is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support children in foster care.
Inspired by Boston CASA's work, Castrichini aims to raise awareness for foster kids' challenges.
Castrichini's involvement with CASA began after attending their gala, inspired by family connections.

title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Over 400 Boston Public School jobs could be cut, affecting high-needs students, as part of a proposed budget plan.
The Boston Teachers Union and protestors demand a $48 million investment, 1% of Boston's budget, to avoid staffing cuts.
Proposed school closures and reconfigurations are expected, with the Boston School Committee voting on a $1.7 billion budget.

title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 160 'No Kings' protests are planned in Massachusetts on March 28, with Boston Common as a key location.
Massachusetts is second to California in the number of 'No Kings' events, part of over 3,000 nationwide demonstrations.
The protests respond to President Trump's policies, including immigration and U.S. involvement in Iran, aiming to bolster democracy.

title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's 'Pardon The Interruption' hosts believe Jaylen Brown should be the NBA MVP frontrunner this season.
Jaylen Brown has averaged 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.1 assists per game, leading Boston in Tatum's absence.
The Celtics are currently second in the Eastern Conference with a 47-24 record, boosting Brown's MVP case.

title: Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Marshfield police are searching for Callahan Pierce, an 8-year-old boy last seen on Moraine Street Tuesday night.
Callahan was wearing a black sweatshirt, black sweatpants, and carrying a backpack at the time of his disappearance.
Police request residents with home cameras check footage and ask for tips at 781-834-6655, advising against visiting the area.

title: Will James Hagens play for the Bruins this season? Don Sweeney responds.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement with the Providence Bruins after leaving college.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney emphasized they won't rush Hagens into NHL games during their playoff push.
Hagens, a top prospect, will play his first pro game for the Providence Bruins on Wednesday.

title: Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, sentenced to life for 1986 murder of Tracy Gilpin, 15, in Plymouth.
Gilpin was reported missing Oct. 1986; body found 20 days later in Myles Standish State Park.
Hand's 2018 interviews led to his arrest; charges of kidnapping and assault dropped in 2019.
